Identification.LongTitle: An Act to establish Leif Erikson Day

Provision.Text: This enactment designates the ninth day of October in each and every year as âLeif Erikson Dayâ.

Whereas Leif Erikson is believed to have been the first European to land in North America, around the year 1000, making him the forerunner of the well-known European explorers that followed and, therefore, the first to open the European exploration of North America;

Whereas there is historical and archaeological evidence to suggest that Leif Erikson established the oldest known Norse settlement in North America, in what is today LâAnse aux Meadows National Historic Site of Canada in the province of Newfoundland and Labrador;
Whereas the people of Canada wish to honour and recognize the significant historical voyage of Leif Erikson, which included landing on the North American continent;
And whereas the people of Canada also wish to honour the valuable contributions of the Scandinavian peoples of Norway, Sweden, Denmark, Finland and Iceland to Canada;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Leif Erikson Day Act.
Throughout Canada, in each and every year, the ninth day of October is to be known as âLeif Erikson Dayâ.
For greater certainty, Leif Erikson Day is not a legal holiday or a non-juridical day.
